domain: an independent film producer's slate and personal life

turns.text: First financing win of the year. A private investor named Aldous Renn agreed to anchor my next feature.
Landing Aldous Renn as an anchor investor is a strong start. How much of the budget does he cover?
He's putting in the lead equity, roughly half the budget. Aldous Renn is the cornerstone of the whole raise.
I'm not running this alone. My co-producer Tamsin Okoro came aboard to handle the line-producing and the crew.
Having Tamsin Okoro on line-producing and crew takes a huge load off you. How do you divide it?
I take the creative and the money side, Tamsin Okoro takes the physical production. We split producer credit evenly.
We finally locked the title. The feature is called Saltmarsh, a slow-burn coastal thriller.
Saltmarsh is an evocative title. Is that the film you're building the whole slate around?
Yeah, Saltmarsh is the flagship. Everything else on my slate is secondary to it.
Setting our premiere strategy. We're aiming Saltmarsh at the Telluride Film Festival for its world premiere.
Telluride Film Festival is a prestige launch. When do submissions close?
Submissions are in the spring. I want Telluride Film Festival even if the edit is tight.
Big call today. A boutique distributor called Lantern Releasing wants first look at Saltmarsh.
Lantern Releasing taking a first look is encouraging. Are they offering anything concrete yet?
Just interest for now. Lantern Releasing handles arthouse titles, which fits us perfectly.
Casting breakthrough. Our lead actor is Marguerite Vale, who read for the fisherman's widow and was extraordinary.
Marguerite Vale in the lead is a real coup. Is she officially attached now?
Signing this week. Marguerite Vale carries the entire third act, so it had to be her.
Brought on a location scout named Boyd Castellan to find our coastline before the budget locks.
A scout like Boyd Castellan early can save you a fortune later. What's his brief?
Find a tidal flat that reads as bleak and remote. Boyd Castellan has three weeks to come back with options.
Adopted a one-eyed rescue greyhound this weekend. Named her Pixel. My partner Soren and I picked her up near our place in Glasgow.
Welcome home, Pixel! A greyhound is a surprisingly low-energy housemate. How is she with the Glasgow apartment?
She's already taken over the only sunny spot in our Glasgow flat. Total diva. Soren is smitten.
Gut punch. Aldous Renn got cold feet and withdrew his equity entirely last night.
Aldous Renn withdrawing the lead equity is a serious hole. What's your gap now?
He pulled the full amount. I'm scrambling to backfill before the bond company panics.
Tamsin Okoro got recognized in a way I didn't expect. She won the Producers Guild Mark of the Year for an earlier film.
Winning the Producers Guild Mark is a major honor. That raises the whole production's profile.
It does. Tamsin Okoro carrying that credential makes the rest of the financing conversations far easier.
The rough cut screened privately and the response floored us. Saltmarsh won the jury's Kestrel Prize at the work-in-progress showcase.
The Kestrel Prize for a work-in-progress cut is huge validation. How was the room?
Silent in the right way, then thunderous. Saltmarsh clearly landed with that audience.
Premiere logistics are firming up. The Telluride Film Festival confirmed its dates and it runs in Mountain Village this year.
Mountain Village for the Telluride Film Festival — that's the heart of it. Are you set for travel?
Booking now. Being in Mountain Village for the premiere means everything for the film's launch.
Deal news. Lantern Releasing closed and they're taking Benelux rights for the theatrical run.
Lantern Releasing locking the Benelux territory is a real commitment. Are other territories still open?
For now they only took Benelux. The rest of the world I'll sell at the festival.
Wonderful news about my lead. Marguerite Vale was just named to the Cannes jury for next year's competition.
Marguerite Vale on the Cannes jury is a real prestige boost for the whole project. How did she take it?
Thrilled and slightly terrified. Marguerite Vale on that jury will draw eyes to our premiere too.
The scout came through. Boyd Castellan found our coastline on the Isle of Harris in the Outer Hebrides.
The Isle of Harris is a stunning, brutal coastline. Did Boyd Castellan lock the permits?
Permits pending, but the Isle of Harris is exactly the bleakness the script needs. Boyd Castellan nailed it.
Life change to match the work. My partner Soren, who works as a freelance colorist, and I are relocating to be near the post house. We're moving to Bristol.
Moving to Bristol with Soren to be near post is a smart consolidation. Is the post house there?
Right downtown. Living in Bristol means I can walk to the edit suite every day, and Soren's freelance gigs are all nearby too.
Soren made it official too. Soren just accepted a staff colorist position at the post house and starts Monday.
Soren going on staff as a colorist at the same post house is convenient and exciting. Same building as your edit?
Same floor. Soren grading our film as a staff colorist keeps the whole thing in the family.
Took Pixel to the harbor for the first time. She watched the boats for an hour, completely transfixed.
A greyhound mesmerized by boats is a lovely image. Did she try to chase any?
Too dignified for that. She just judged the seagulls and refused to walk home.

queries.question: What happened to the equity from my anchor investor Aldous Renn?
Which industry award did my co-producer Tamsin Okoro win?
queries.answer: Producers Guild Mark of the Year
accepted_forms: Producers Guild Mark of the Year

queries.question: What prize did the film Saltmarsh win at the work-in-progress showcase?

accepted_forms: Isle of Harris in the Outer Hebrides
